ops.title: daemonkit drain engine: ownership contracts (post-review)
ops.body:
**The drain/ package (commit bc91e422)** implements drain-on-upgrade. Consumers (fusekit Track F first) implement the `Resources` seam on top. The load-bearing invariants, hardened by a two-lens adversarial review (fable ordering + opus concurrency caught 1 high + 3 ownership defects, all fixed + regression-tested):

**Liveness is tri-state** (`Alive`/`Dead`/`Undetermined`, zero-value = Undetermined). A probe/stat/enumeration failure or unreadable identity → Undetermined, and every force/reap/adopt gate treats it as *do nothing*. `ForcePolicy` zero-value = `ForcePolicyDefer` (never force); only `ForcePolicyConfirmedDead` permits force, on carcass proof.

**Transition normative order** (single-ownership proven after EVERY step by crash-injection tests): draining flag → stop AND close intake BEFORE snapshot → settle running+queued → snapshot journal → TRUNCATE canonical → bind drain listener → release flock → spawn. Intake closes before snapshot (a paused handler can't register post-snapshot); canonical is truncated (every row lives in exactly one journal). `snapshot()` errors before Truncate if any row was CAS-suppressed (stale reused generation journal = fail loud). owner.json is written BEFORE rows (rows-exist ⇒ owner-readable, pinned by an intra-snapshot crash test).

**AdoptDead is admitted under the Intake** — the fix for the high-severity race: a death-scan adoption's canonical write + generation-dir removal is one admitted unit, so `Transition`'s Settle barrier joins any in-flight adoption before the snapshot, and an adoption after Close returns `ErrDraining` leaving the source generation intact. Without this, adoption could straddle snapshot/Truncate and lose resources permanently.

**Journal**: monotone-seq CAS (`Apply` applies only incoming.seq > stored.seq; stale replay is a no-op). `Truncate` preserves the seq epoch via a reserved `~seq` high-water key — a post-Truncate `Bump` strictly exceeds any pre-Truncate seq, so a stale replay can't out-rank a fresh registration. Disk-backed under `<dotdir>/drain/<gen>/`, foreign keys byte-preserved.

**Resources seam**: `Keys/AttestIdle/Seize/Yield/Restore`; `Fence` interface (`Held()`/`Release()`) satisfied by fusekit's `*lease.Fence`. Fence held through teardown AND handoff. Any busy attest / wedged teardown / lost fence / handoff failure ABORTS the key's sweep and calls Restore — never advances the journal row, never force-clears. But a journal-advance failure AFTER a successful Yield does NOT Restore (that would steal the resource from the successor) — the drainer dies loudly, a later scan adopts the carcass.

**Run**: per-peer breakers on `proc.Backoff`; disk-backed `StrikeStore` (3/10min, persisted ladder, survives restart). **ScanPeers** runs unconditionally before accepting registrations (cold-start hole) + periodically; adoption needs a complete death scan + revalidated identity (unreadable peer stays unadopted). **Simple** (request daemons): admission = frame receipt (queued-behind-mutex counts in-flight); order stop→drain running+queued→closing→cancel; content-hash dedupe via atomic O_CREATE|O_EXCL stamp, fails open. `daemon.SkewWatch.OnSkew` wires `drain.OnSkew` → Transition. See [[daemonkit-trust-spike-verdict-purego-viable-cgo-free]].
